alerts.title: Iran enrichment reaches 60% U-235; breakout timeline 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: Iran's uranium enrichment level remains at 60% U-235 with estimated nuclear breakout time of 1-2 weeks per global_metrics. IAEA reports Iran barred inspectors for nearly one year and flagged proliferation risk. Multiple nuclear headlines confirm escalating concern over enriched uranium stockpiles and undisclosed transfers amid active Persian Gulf hostilities.

alerts.title: Houthis kill 3 in Red Sea merchant vessel attack; escalation ongoing
alerts.summary: Yemen's Houthi rebels conducted fatal attack on commercial shipping in Red Sea (lat 15.7, lon 40.77), killing 3 crew members per incidents feed (Courthouse News). Multiple sources (AP News, BBC, Times of Israel) report renewed Houthi missile strikes on both Red Sea shipping and Israel, with forecasts of further disruption to global maritime commerce. Pattern indicates sustained proxy escalation despite ceasefire talks.

alerts.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire violations widen; 9+ killed in continued strikes
alerts.summary: Despite June 3 ceasefire agreement, Israeli drone and airstrike operations continue across southern Lebanon and Beirut vicinity (lat 33.62-33.87, lon 35.46-36.04). Nine confirmed deaths reported including paramedic; two additional casualties from separate strike on Syrians and Bangladeshis per incidents feed (Middle East Eye, Al Jazeera). Hezbollah leadership rejects ceasefire absent Israeli withdrawal, signaling agreement breakdown.

alerts.title: Threat level escalates to critical; Trump administration balancing Iran response
alerts.summary: US sentiment remains elevated at 51% negative vs. 42% positive, with 'Trump' as dominant keyword (65 mentions) alongside 'war,' 'Bolton,' and 'unpatriotic' per sentiment feed. Telegram engagement on ceasefire breaches and Kuwait airport strike remains high (~44K-49K views, ME Spectator). Strategic moment reflects internal US debate over restraint vs. escalation amid nuclear timeline pressure.

alerts.title: Three US carrier strike groups positioned across Middle East theaters
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower deployed Red Sea (lat 15.15, lon 42.32), USS Abraham Lincoln in Arabian Sea (lat 20.67, lon 60.16), and USS Gerald R. Ford in Mediterranean (lat 33.66, lon 29.99) per assets feed. Positioning coincides with active Houthi Red Sea strikes, ongoing Iran-US Gulf exchange, and Israel-Lebanon ceasefire violations, suggesting sustained deterrence posture amid 1-2 week nuclear breakout window.

analysis_note: Iran approaches nuclear breakout within 1-2 weeks while proxy escalation (Houthis, Hezbollah) continues despite ceasefire agreements; US maintains regional military presence amid Trump administration debate over restraint vs. kinetic response.
